data.title: 96% of Quantum Funding Is Concentrated in 45 Global Hubs. Here's What It Means for Quantum Stocks
data.description:
Quantum computing is not spreading across the globe. Instead, it is flowing into a short list of tight hubs. This is according to a new study from the European Centre for International Political Economy. The study finds that 96% of global quantum company funding now goes to just 45 dense "quantum clusters." That is up from 92% only two years ago. In other words, more money is going to fewer places.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The report says the future of quantum "will not be decided country by country, but cluster by cluster." That line sums up the shift. Investors tracking the space should focus less on nations and more on cities.

To qualify as a full cluster, a region must host at least two startups with $10 million in combined funding, or one startup with $25 million or more in funding. It must also have at least five active research, industry, or state groups working in quantum. By that test, only 45 regions make the cut.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Booking (BKNG) Earnings Tomorrow – Which KPI Should You Watch?
data.description:
American travel and reservation company Booking Holdings (BKNG) will release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results after the market closes tomorrow, February 18. Investors should closely monitor trends in Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), as they highlight past performance and set expectations for the future. For Booking.com, Gross Travel Bookings stands out as the vital KPI, directly fueling revenue from customer bookings.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What Is a KPI?

A KPI is a measurable metric, like revenue growth, margins, or daily active users (DAUs), that offers insights into trends, efficiency, and potential challenges such as market shifts or resource inefficiencies.

Why Gross Bookings Matter for BKNG

Gross Bookings tracks the total value of all travel services booked by customers (net of cancellations), signaling consumer demand and market share. The company divides its Gross Bookings into two segments:

  • Merchant Bookings: BKNG collects full payment upfront and pays suppliers (net). This model offers higher risk, more control, and better margins.
  • Agency Bookings: BKNG earns commissions only, as suppliers handle payments. This is lower risk, less control, and easier to scale.

In Q3FY25, the company's Gross Bookings increased 14% year-over-year to $49.7 billion. The company had guided for 11%–13% growth in Q4.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Micron (MU) Jumps 44% YTD as AI Memory Shortage Lifts Margins to 68%
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) is spending huge sums to grow its chip output as AI demand keeps rising. The company is building new factories to meet what may be the tightest memory supply market in decades. In Boise, Idaho, Micron is investing $50 billion to expand its main campus and add two new chip plants. The first factory is set to begin making silicon wafers in mid-2027. Both plants should be running by the end of 2028.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, Micron has started work on a $100 billion factory site near Syracuse, New York. It also plans major growth abroad, including a $9.6 billion project in Japan. Meanwhile, rivals like SK Hynix (HK:7709) and Samsung (SSNLF) are also adding supply as the race for AI memory heats up.

Meanwhile, MU shares dropped slightly yesterday, closing at $411.66. However, the stock climbed over 44% since the turn of the year.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Goldman Sachs (GS) Revises DEI Policy as Legal and Political Risks Reshape Corporate DEI
data.description:
Goldman Sachs Group (GS) is set to remove race, gender identity, and sexual orientation from the list of factors it uses when looking for new board members. According to a Wall Street Journal report, the move comes after pressure from a small but active shareholder group and marks another step back from formal DEI language across Wall Street.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The change does not mean the bank will stop seeking a mix of views and life paths. However, it does mean that certain identity-based terms will no longer appear in its official board criteria.

Meanwhile, GS shares rose slightly on Monday, closing at $905.14.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Will Activist Pressure Revive TripAdvisor Stock (TRIP) as Starboard Plans a Major Board Overhaul?
data.description:
Activist investor Starboard Value plans to push for a major shake-up of TripAdvisor's (TRIP) board, the Wall Street Journal reported. TRIP stock plunged 15% on Thursday after the online travel platform reported disappointing fourth-quarter results. Fears of the potential impact of artificial intelligence (AI) on the company's business have also impacted investor sentiment. Overall, TRIP stock has declined about 46% over the past year.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

TripAdvisor Faces Activist Pressure

Starboard is reportedly gearing up to nominate the majority of directors on TripAdvisor's board, which comprises eight members. According to a letter viewed by the Wall Street Journal and sources familiar with the matter, the Jeff Smith-led activist investor now has more than a 9% stake in the company. Starboard is expected to send a letter to TripAdvisor's board on Tuesday morning.

In July 2025, the Wall Street Journal reported Starboard's stake in TripAdvisor. The stake was worth $160 million at that time. The activist investor pushed for efforts to improve the profitability of the company's Viator platform and its namesake brand. Moreover, Starboard asked the company to consider selling TheFork, its restaurant reservation tool.

Last week, TripAdvisor reported adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $0.04 for Q4 2025, significantly missing the Street's estimate of $0.15. Moreover, the company's Q4 adjusted EPS declined 87% year-over-year. The company said it is considering strategic options for TheFork as it aims to focus on the rapidly growing experiences segment and unlock shareholder value.

Is TRIP Stock a Good Buy?

Given the ongoing challenges, Wall Street has a Hold consensus rating on TripAdvisor stock based on five Holds, three Buys, and three Sells. The average TRIP stock price target of $14.72 indicates 53.2% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall as Fears over AI Disruption Persist
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were in the red early Tuesday as concerns about artificial intelligence (AI) disrupting several sectors, including software, real estate, and financial services, continued to weigh on investor sentiment. The U.S. stock market was closed on Monday in observance of Presidents' Day.

Last week, all three major indexes ended in losses. The S&P 500 and the Dow Jones fell more than 1%, while the Nasdaq Composite declined over 2%. Notably, the Nasdaq Composite has ended in the negative for five consecutive weeks.

data.title: M&A News: Danaher Nears $10B Deal for Masimo
data.description:
American healthcare company Danaher (DHR) is nearing a $9.9 billion acquisition of medical technology firm Masimo (MASI). The deal reflects a hefty premium to Masimo's closing market cap of $6.99 billion on Friday. The Financial Times first reported the story, noting a deal announcement could come today barring last-minute snags.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

MASI shares have dropped over 28% in the past year, while DHR is up nearly 4%. The purchase would rank among Danaher's largest since its $21.4 billion Cytiva buyout in 2020 from GE Life Sciences. Masimo is also embroiled in a long-standing patent fight with Apple (AAPL) over Apple Watch technology.

What the Two Companies Do

Danaher is among the top global innovators in life sciences, biotech, and diagnostics, delivering tools and services to drive progress in healthcare, scientific research, and pharmaceutical development.

Masimo specializes in noninvasive patient monitoring technologies (like pulse oximeters), medical devices, and sensors to improve patient outcomes and reduce care costs. Two years ago, activist Politan Capital Management overhauled its board in a bitter proxy fight, ousting founder Joe Kiani from chairman and CEO roles. Kiani still holds about 5% of Masimo and Politan owns about 9%.

Is DHR a Good Long-Term Investment?

Analysts remain highly optimistic about Danaher's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, DHR stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 17 Buys and one Hold rating. The average Danaher price target of $266.18 implies 25.2% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Norwegian Cruise Line Braces for Activist Pressure after Elliott's 10% Stake
data.description:
Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) braces for an activist battle after Elliott Investment Management disclosed a more than 10% stake in the struggling cruise operator. According to the Wall Street Journal, the activist investor is looking for a full turnaround, including a proxy fight. Elliott cited Norwegian's constant underperformance versus rivals Royal Caribbean (RCL) and Carnival (CCL), who have rebounded strongly after the COVID-19 slump.

Norwegian is the fourth-largest cruise operator globally, with premium brands under its umbrella. NCLH stock has lost 3.7% year-to-date and has trailed the S&P 500 (SPX) index with a 21% decline over the past year. Despite recovering consumer demand, shares trade near pandemic-era levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Musk Reaffirms Tesla 'Cybercab Starts Production in April'
data.description:
Elon Musk, CEO of electric vehicle (EV) maker Tesla (TSLA), has reaffirmed plans to begin production of the most-awaited "no pedals, no steering wheel" Cybercab in April 2026. This marks the third time in six months that Musk has cited this timeline, although he is known for making bold promises that often face delays.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In December 2025, Musk said Tesla was "testing the production system," with a "real production ramp" in April. He repeated it on January 23 and again yesterday, adding in a follow-up post that read "10 years ago, Elon Musk predicted cars would later have no steering wheels: the audience laughed."

Why the Cybercab Is Special

Tesla's Cybercab is central to Musk's robotaxi vision: a two-passenger, pedal-free, steering-free autonomous vehicle priced under $30,000, designed for ride-hailing fleets. This shifts Tesla from EV sales to high-margin services, targeting a $10 trillion robotaxi market, as per Musk. Paired with the humanoid Optimus robot, it positions Tesla as an AI and robotics leader. However, regulatory approvals from National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) and Full Self-Driving (FSD) reliability remain hurdles, especially after Tesla's history of missed autonomy deadlines.

In January, Musk noted production will follow an S-curve due mostly to new parts for the Cybercab and the Optimus. He echoed the same timeline yesterday, stating initial production rates will be "agonizingly slow" but ramp to "insanely fast." He reiterated that the Cybercab's radical redesign aims for ~5x higher output, starting slow before surging.

Is Tesla a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Analysts remain cautious about Tesla's long-term prospects due to sluggish EV sales. On TipRanks, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $396.80 implies 4.9% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA shares have gained 17.9%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
